questions.question: What is the purpose of <!DOCTYPE html>?
options: B) Tells the browser to render the document in standards mode as HTML5
questions.correctAnswer: B) Tells the browser to render the document in standards mode as HTML5

questions.question: Why is semantic HTML preferred over generic div-heavy markup?
options: B) It improves meaning, accessibility, maintainability, and often SEO
C) It reduces HTML size to zero
questions.correctAnswer: B) It improves meaning, accessibility, maintainability, and often SEO

questions.question: Which element should represent the main unique content of a page?
questions.correctAnswer: B) main

questions.question: What is the difference between section and article?
options: B) article is self-contained independent content; section groups related thematic content
C) section is only for sidebars
questions.correctAnswer: B) article is self-contained independent content; section groups related thematic content

questions.question: Why should every form input typically have a label?
options: B) It improves accessibility and click target behavior by associating text with the control
C) It is required only for submit buttons
D) It stores the input value
questions.correctAnswer: B) It improves accessibility and click target behavior by associating text with the control

questions.question: What does the alt attribute on an img element do?
options: B) Provides alternative text for accessibility and fallback when image cannot be displayed
questions.correctAnswer: B) Provides alternative text for accessibility and fallback when image cannot be displayed

questions.question: When should alt be empty (alt="")?
options: B) For purely decorative images that convey no meaningful content
questions.correctAnswer: B) For purely decorative images that convey no meaningful content

questions.question: What is the role of the head element?
options: A) Contains all visible page content
B) Holds metadata like title, meta tags, links to stylesheets, and scripts
questions.correctAnswer: B) Holds metadata like title, meta tags, links to stylesheets, and scripts

questions.question: Why is the title element important?
options: A) It styles the page heading
B) It sets the browser/tab title and contributes to search result context
questions.correctAnswer: B) It sets the browser/tab title and contributes to search result context

questions.question: What is the purpose of the meta viewport tag?
options: B) Controlling layout viewport behavior on mobile devices
questions.correctAnswer: B) Controlling layout viewport behavior on mobile devices

questions.question: What is the difference between block and inline elements in HTML by default?
options: A) Inline elements always have margins
B) Block elements typically start on a new line; inline elements flow within text
C) Block elements cannot contain text
D) Inline elements cannot contain links
questions.correctAnswer: B) Block elements typically start on a new line; inline elements flow within text

questions.question: Why should table markup include th and sometimes scope attributes?
options: B) To provide header semantics that assist screen readers in understanding relationships
questions.correctAnswer: B) To provide header semantics that assist screen readers in understanding relationships

questions.question: What is the purpose of fieldset and legend in forms?
options: A) They are deprecated layout tools
B) They semantically group related controls and provide an accessible group label
questions.correctAnswer: B) They semantically group related controls and provide an accessible group label

questions.question: When is ARIA most appropriate?
options: A) Always instead of semantic HTML
B) When native HTML semantics are insufficient and you must enhance accessibility carefully
questions.correctAnswer: B) When native HTML semantics are insufficient and you must enhance accessibility carefully

questions.question: Why is nesting interactive elements like a button inside an anchor problematic?
options: B) It creates invalid/ambiguous interaction behavior and accessibility issues
D) It is required for navigation
questions.correctAnswer: B) It creates invalid/ambiguous interaction behavior and accessibility issues

questions.question: What is the benefit of using button instead of div for clickable actions?
options: B) Buttons provide built-in keyboard and accessibility behavior appropriate for actions
C) div is more semantic for actions
D) Buttons only work in forms
questions.correctAnswer: B) Buttons provide built-in keyboard and accessibility behavior appropriate for actions

questions.question: What does the picture element solve?
options: B) It enables art direction or responsive image source selection via nested source elements
C) It replaces all img elements
D) It provides lazy loading only
questions.correctAnswer: B) It enables art direction or responsive image source selection via nested source elements

questions.question: Why is lang attribute on the html element important?
options: A) It changes font rendering only
B) It helps assistive tech and browsers understand the document language
questions.correctAnswer: B) It helps assistive tech and browsers understand the document language

questions.question: What is progressive enhancement in HTML-centric development?
options: B) Starting with robust semantic HTML and enhancing with CSS/JS where available
questions.correctAnswer: B) Starting with robust semantic HTML and enhancing with CSS/JS where available

questions.question: What is the practical difference between id and class?
options: B) id should be unique in the document; class is reusable across multiple elements
C) class can be used only once
D) id is only for CSS
questions.correctAnswer: B) id should be unique in the document; class is reusable across multiple elements

questions.question: Which tag is used for hyperlink in HTML?
questions.correctAnswer: B) <a>

questions.question: Which tag is used for image?
questions.correctAnswer: A)
